response.description_long: Lifecycle messaging for e-commerce is plagued by attribution theater — every tool claims revenue the store would have earned anyway. Relaymark automates welcome series, abandoned-cart recovery, win-back campaigns, and post-purchase flows across email and SMS, and measures what actually works. The product connects to Shopify and BigCommerce, building a unified profile for every shopper from orders, browsing, cart, and message interaction data. A visual journey builder allows brands to trigger, wait, branch, and send based on profile segments and live behavior — no code required. Send-time optimization picks each shopper's most engaging hour, and a holdout system keeps a control group per flow to report true incremental revenue rather than last-click attribution. Over 900 brands run on Relaymark. The template library, organized by vertical and job, ships with branching logic and timing intact. The most-copied template is a 4-message abandoned-cart series. SMS is handled with consent, quiet hours, frequency caps, and automatic opt-out; email and SMS coordinate inside one journey. Deliverability is productized with dedicated sending infrastructure, list hygiene, warm-up schedules, and a specialist reviewing each new account's first month. Beyond Shopify and BigCommerce, Relaymark syncs audiences to Meta and Google for suppression and lookalikes, and streams events via webhooks to data warehouses. Review-request flows integrate with Okendo and Judge.me. Relaymark was founded in 2020 with a team of 30 people, fully remote, and customers concentrated in fashion, beauty, and food & beverage. The founders were retention managers, deliverability engineers, and lifecycle marketers who ran these programs by hand at brands and agencies. Pricing scales with contactable profiles; email volume is included, SMS is metered separately. A free tier covers stores under 500 profiles. Annual billing discounts are available. Migration help is included and typically completed within two weeks. Relaymark treats lifecycle messaging as infrastructure rather than a campaign calendar, with branching on commerce data, holdout measurement, and account-managed deliverability as key differentiators from generic ESPs.
